rationale: The main residential roof is a large, complex hip-roof structure with two primary volumes forming an L/cross-shaped plan. Bounding box for the NE body was ~285×215 px and the SW wing ~255×235 px, with ~140×75 px of overlap subtracted, yielding ~110,700 sq px total. Converted at 0.038269 sq ft/sq px gives ~4,237 sq ft; trimmed ~15% for edge irregularities to arrive at ~3,600 sq ft. The roof is predominantly hip-style with no visible gable/rake edges. Ridge lines were traced along the two main peaks (~210 px NE + ~135 px SW). Approximately 10 hip lines were identified at corners/ends, averaging ~130 px each. Three valley junctions were identified where the two volumes intersect. Eave perimeter was estimated from all lower horizontal edges around both sections. Confidence is moderate (0.4) due to roof complexity, oblique shadows, and difficulty precisely delineating volume boundaries from overhead imagery.
